characters.description: "Renegade" Bad JanetBioStatusSpeciesMarbelizedBad JanetBirthdayAgeUnknownInfinityReboots40,000,000Full nameBad JanetNickname(s)Janet(disguised)Renegade Bad Janet(by this wiki)GenderEyesHairGenderlessDark brownDark brownFromJanet WarehouseResidenceThe Bad PlaceThe VoidMichael's Neighborhood Improvement Experiment(formerly)OccupationSaboteur(for both sides)Walking databaseRelationshipsFamilyJanet(sister)Neutral Janet(sister)Disco Janet(sister)RomancesNoneFriendsJanetJasonTahaniShawn(formerly)AffiliationThe Bad Place(formerly)Soul SquadJanetProductionPortrayed byD'Arcy CardenFirst seenChapter 40: A Girl From Arizona Part 1Last seenChapter 48: The AnswerAppears inChapter 40: A Girl From Arizona Part 1Chapter 41: A Girl From Arizona Part 2Chapter 42: ChillaxingChapter 43: Tinker, Tailor, Demon, SpyChapter 45: A Chip Driver MysteryChapter 47: The Funeral To End All FuneralsChapter 48: The Answer Renegade Bad Janet(by this wiki) The VoidMichael's Neighborhood Improvement Experiment(formerly) Bad Janet impersonates Good Janet in an attempt to sabotagethe experiment. When caught, she is imprisoned by Michael for several months before he frees her with a copy ofJanet and Michael's manifesto. After reading it, she converts to their side and mobilizes all other Janets in existence for a Hail Mary play to buy time for theSoul Squad.

characters.name: Acidcat
characters.description: AcidcatBioStatusSpeciesdeceasedHumanGenderMaleResidenceJacksonville, FloridaProductionPortrayed byDave FergusonFirst seenChapter 4: Jason MendozaAcidic felineis a character seen in flashback in the series The Good Place. He is a DJ who wears a giant cartoon cat's head when he performs. Jason Mendoza once gave Acidcat a demo of his own EDM, which Acidcat dismissed as terrible, but offered Jason the opportunity to wear the cat's head and stand in for him while he went to a party. Jason grabbed the opportunity, only to then mess it up by playing his own music instead of Acidcat's. Acidcat's brand is based off of the popular DJ and EDM producer Deadmau5, his helmet having the biggest resemblance, sporting an extremely similar shape to deadmau5' helmet, with minor changes to the ears, the addition of a nose and whiskers and bio-hazard symbols on the eyes

characters.name: Andy
characters.description: AndyBioProductionPortrayed byGeorge BasilFirst seenChapter 3: Tahani Al-JamilAndyis a character seen in a flashback toEleanor's life on Earth in Chapter 3 ofThe Good Place. He is a local cafe owner exposed as a pervert who inappropriately touches women who apply for jobs there. Eleanor's then-boyfriendSamuelis shocked by this and says Eleanor should stop buying her coffee there, but Eleanor is not concerned.

characters.name: Architect
characters.description: Architectsare types ofImmortal Beingswho reside in the Good and Bad Place headquarters. An Architect is an occupation rather than a type of being, as Immortal Beings can be promoted to Architect (asMichaelwas). Architects design Good and Bad Placeneighborhoodsfor humans to reside in.Mindy'sneighborhoodwas designed by The Good Place before The Bad Place modified it. When a human dies, their file is sent from theAccounting Departmentto The Bad Place or The Good Place. There, that human's neighborhood's architect must incorporate that human, among the other humans in that neighborhood, into the design. The Architect will then run the neighborhood by their boss, and if it is approved, they will be able to build it. Architects build their neighborhood with the help of that neighborhood'sJanet, and then the Janet will typically welcome the residents. The Architects have full control over the building process, and the Janets cannot argue, even if they do notunderstand, as when Michael and Janet were building Neighborhood 12358W.

characters.name: Bad Janet
characters.description: Bad JanetsFirst seenChapter 9: ...Someone Like Me as a MemberTypical ResidenceThe Bad PlaceSubspecies ofJanetsTypical LifespanEternalAppearanceA woman dressed in a leather jacket and black clothingRole in UniverseHelper of demons Bad Janetis a character inThe Good Placeand is played byD'Arcy Carden. Bad Janet is a sentient database for The Bad Place (presumably) created by "the Makers of Light, Darkness, and Everything", much like herGood Placecounterpart. Janet is able to instantly appear to anyone's side when they call their name (though unlike her Good Place Counterpart, always on the opposite side than the one who summoned them is looking, forcing them to turn around). Bad Janet by default is rude, offensive and possesses a very antisocial attitude towards others. They are incapable of being nice, and before their reboots, would melt upon imitating a Good Janet. When asked a question, they usually insult the asker and goes to their void with no help. They are always on their cell phone even when doing other things like casually opening the door to Michael's cell. They are also known for their quintessential fart noise, which is their way of showing apathy. However, Bad Janet did show some alliance with their 'sister', Good Janet. They disobeyed Shawn, saying the whole Afterlife system was 'royally effed', and mobilized literally all other Janets to preventJudge Genfrom destroyingEarth. As callous as they may be, Bad Janet is not fully a villain.

characters.name: Bambadjan
characters.description: BambadjanBioAgeUnknownFull nameBambadjanGenderMale (human form)ResidenceThe Bad PlaceOccupationBad Place TorturerProductionPortrayed byBambadjan BambaFirst seenChapter 1: Everything is Fine Bambadjan was one of the 318 torturers (demons) used byMichaelin his "experiment". In Version 1, he posed as a rights activist and helped tortureEleanor. He was very important in the final stages of Version 1 where he pretended to help Eleanor find a way to stay out of the Bad Place, he told her that he found calling the otherEleanorReal Eleanor helpful. After Eleanor found out that they were in the bad place he rushed in and said that he found a way to save everyone from the Bad Place but was sent away by Eleanor. He was part ofShawn's team inThe Selection, and he suggested Joseph Stalin for the experiment, but his idea was rejected.

characters.description: BartBioGenderMale (human form)ProductionPortrayed byJohn HartmannFirst seenChapter 5: Category 55 Emergency Doomsday CrisisBartis a recurring character inThe Good Place. He is introduced asNina's soulmate and an expert in identity fraud duringChapter 5: Category 55 Doomsday Crisiswhere he torturesEleanor ShellstropandChidi Anagonyeby making them think that they could be found out for Eleanor not belonging in the Good Place, but is later revealed to be a Bad Place torturer. In Attempt #2, he goes by the aliasEd.

characters.name: Betsy
characters.description: BetsyBioStatusSpeciesPresumed deceasedHumanGenderFemaleResidencePhoenix, ArizonaProductionPortrayed byAllyn RachelFirst seenChapter 2: FlyingBetsyis a character appearing in flashbacks in the seriesThe Good Place. She was one ofEleanor Shellstrop's co-workers, but the two had a somewhat belligerent relationship, despite Betsy's efforts to be friendly toward her, such as inviting her along on nights out drinking, specifically on Thursdays (Eleanor accepted, but always shirked the responsibility of being designated driver), and bringing her a cake on her birthday (which Eleanor turned down flat). She kicked Eleanor out of designated driving and tried to make sure that she couldn't cheat the system beforehand. Nearing the ending of Chapter 2: Flying, Eleanor informs Betsy and the others also going to Thursday Night Drinks that she will be having intercourse with the bartender. This would mean Eleanor wouldn't be able to drive the others home; and in response, Betsy offers an ultimatum. Eleanor declines optimistically, and we can infer that she was banned from Thursday Night Drinks by Betsy indefinitely.

characters.name: Brent Norwalk
characters.description: Brent NorwalkBioStatusSpeciesDeceasedHumanBirthdayAgeCirca. 1960s50s (before death)Reboots15,726Full nameBrent NorwalkAKAChip DriverGenderEyesHairMaleHazelBrownFromUnnamed Chicago SuburbResidenceArchitect's Office,testingMichael's Neighborhood Improvement Experiment(formerly)OccupationMaterialsSchoolPrincetonRelationshipsFamily(Unnamed spouse)(Unnamed son)(Unnamed father)(Unnamed grandfather)Romances(Unnamed spouse)ProductionPortrayed byBen KoldykeFirst seenChapter 40: A Girl From Arizona Part 1Last seenChapter 52: Whenever You're Ready Michael's Neighborhood Improvement Experiment(formerly) Brent Norwalkis a series regular in season 4 ofThe Good Place, portrayed by Ben Koldyke. He is Subject Four of the four humans selected for theexperiment. In life, Brent was a casually racist, sexist and all-around self-absorbed CEO of a plastics manufacturing company he inherited from his father and grandfather. He died when he won a game of shots in a Diamond Elite Club Bar with a pilot, and he drunkenly asked if he could fly his helicopter, which resulted in killing them both.

Cat Pash is a spoonerism of the Australian tennis player Pat Cash.

characters.name: Chef Patricia
characters.description: Chef PatriciaBioSpeciesImmortal Being(demon)Full namePatricia Camarillo (probably)RelationshipsAffiliationMichael(formerly)The Bad PlaceProductionPortrayed byJamie DenboAppears inChapter 4: Jason MendozaChef Patriciais a minor character inThe Good Place. She appears in Chapter 4, whenTahani Al-Jamilis asked to organize the opening night of her new restaurant "The Good Plates." The Bad Place In Chapter 5, Patricia Camarillo, who is probably the same person as Chef Patricia, is the person listed 14th in the Neighborhood Rankings, one place ahead ofChidi Anagonye.
